Circling back. Spendflo cut manual testing by 50% using Testsigma, which freed their team to focus on new coverage.

For services firms scaling QA, that efficiency multiplies across every client engagement.

If cutting manual testing in half would help, what day works for a quick look?

Rob

touch_3.subject: QA standardization at scale
touch_3.body:
Hey Heleen,

IT services teams managing QA across dozens of client projects usually struggle to standardize when each project uses a different tech stack.

CRED hit 90% regression automation and 5x faster execution using plain English test creation that works across any tech stack.

If 5x faster regression would help standardize delivery, what day works for a quick chat?

Rob

touch_1.subject: Integration regression at Lucid
touch_1.body:
Hey Shantel,

With 200+ integrations across Lucidchart, Lucidspark, and Lucidscale, what takes more QA time, keeping the integration tests stable or regression on the real-time collaboration features?

Visual collaboration platforms usually see integration maintenance grow faster than the team as partners update their APIs. Each API change requires test rewrites.

CRED hit 90% regression automation and 5x faster execution using Testsigma while managing dozens of third-party integrations.

What day works to see how they kept integration coverage tight while cutting execution by 5x?

Rob

touch_2.subject: Still on my mind
touch_2.body:
Hey Shantel,

Circling back. Medibuddy automated 2,500 tests and cut maintenance in half while scaling their platform integration coverage.

With 200+ integration partners, that maintenance reduction probably compounds per sprint.

If halving maintenance would help, what day works for a quick look?

Rob

touch_3.subject: Lucid platform testing
touch_3.body:
Hey Shantel,

SaaS platforms with 200+ integrations usually spend more time fixing tests broken by partner API changes than building new coverage.

Freshworks reduced flakiness at scale using Testsigma across their massive integration-heavy platform footprint.

If reducing test flakiness would free time for new feature testing, what day works for a quick chat?

Rob
